title: armeria maritima subsp. depilata
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. depilata, also known as the sea thrift or sea pink, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the primrose family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. Armeria maritima subsp. depilata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
